Here are the results:

**There are 4 Linear issues that are "done" but NOT closed by any merged GitHub PR:**

| Linear Issue | Title | Assignee | Team |
|---|---|---|---|
| **ENG-122** | Primary DB failover is slow | heidi | mobile |
| **ENG-125** | Image uploads time out over 5MB | erin | api |
| **ENG-131** | Primary DB failover is slow | mallory | api |
| **ENG-142** | Primary DB failover is slow | niaj | infra |

**ENG-134** (Rate limiter false positives — follow-up) is excluded because merged PR #1024 has `closes_linear: "ENG-134"`, so it is properly accounted for. The other four are "done" in Linear but no merged PR references them — they may have been completed without a PR, marked done manually, or the PR that resolved them didn't set the `closes_linear` field.
I'll start by fetching both the done Linear issues and all merged pull requests to cross-reference

text: Seems the REPL has some state issue. Let me try in Lisp — a clean slate.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def done (linear__list_issues {:state "done"}))
(def prs (github__list_pull_requests {:state "merged"}))
(def done-ids (set (map :id done)))
(def merged-refs (set (keep :closes_linear prs)))
(def bad (sort (clojure.set/difference done-ids merged-refs)))
{:count (count bad) :issues bad}"}

(def prs (github__list_pull_requests {:state "merged"}))
(def done-ids (set (map :id done)))
